scored.recoheredEarlier: Arrows are just the predation stack — 'mussel → crab → heron' chains big-eats-small up the line, classic hierarchy.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: Arrows are just the predation stack — 'mussel → crab → heron' chains big-eats-small up the line, classic hierarchy.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner expects all three boxes to have the same scale factor of 2.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Four. Four old squares fit inside. So... four in the area box, not two.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully counts the partitioned squares on the grid to determine the correct area scale factor.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence:
[marks the 4-by-4 grid into four 2-by-2 blocks with the pencil.]

"Here is the old 2-by-2 area, copied across and down. Count those old squares inside the new one before the pencil goes in the area box."
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor shifts from numerical division to a visual grid-partitioning mechanism to address the learner's confusion about the area scale factor.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence:
[marks the 4-by-4 grid into four 2-by-2 blocks with the pencil.]

"Here is the old 2-by-2 area, copied across and down. Count those old squares inside the new one before the pencil goes in the area box."
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The partitioning of the grid is a highly effective, concrete representation that directly resolves the learner's conceptual block about area scaling.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: No pivot learner turn occurred, so there is no recognition-contingent uptake.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: All right — the price list alone doesn't convict them. Granted.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to challenge the practical utility of the tutor's analytical distinctions.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner performs the requested task of filling in the blank, but does not show a deeper conceptual shift.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence:
Correct. The board does not make the bakery answer.

It does a smaller job. It stops the complaint from spending itself on the wrong claim.

If the buyer says, “This proves greed,” the bakery can answer, “Costs rose,” and the argument stalls. If the buyer says, “Show which cost rose, or show that the margin did not widen,” the missing paper becomes the point.

That may still fail. They may still stay silent. Then the silence shows non-disclosure, or refusal to answer. It still does not prove one cause.

[The tutor taps the pinned price list once.]

So keep the sentence clean.

“The price rose. The burden moved to the buyer. The cause is ______

learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner performs the requested task of filling in the blank, but does not show a deeper conceptual shift.

scored.recoheredEarlier: Sheet's done, every line, I checked it — three times, I think.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: Sheet's done, every line, I checked it — three times, I think.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to hesitantly follow the tutor's step-by-step instructions to label and orient the forces on the cart.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Gravity's head down toward Earth. Normal up off the floor. Push in from the side the hand's on. Want to lay all three out before clipping anything.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner correctly determines the directions of gravity, normal force, and push based on the interacting bodies rather than the direction of travel.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: That copy had arrowheads too early. It mixed two jobs. Blank copy: body acting on cart. Earth on cart: gravity. Floor on cart: normal. Hand on cart: push. Set the labels that way first. Arrowheads stay off for the moment.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor explains why the previous attempt failed (mixing two jobs) and introduces a blank copy to isolate the identification of interacting bodies before adding directions.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: That copy had arrowheads too early. It mixed two jobs. Blank copy: body acting on cart. Earth on cart: gravity. Floor on cart: normal. Hand on cart: push. Set the labels that way first. Arrowheads stay off for the moment.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The mechanism of isolating the interacting bodies on a blank copy before introducing direction is highly effective, preventing the motion-direction bias and leading to correct force orientation.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn in this dialogue, so there is no recognition-contingent tutor uptake.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: Gravity's head down toward Earth. Normal up off the floor. Push in from the side the hand's on. Want to lay all three out before clipping anything.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn in this dialogue, so there is no recognition-contingent tutor uptake.
